_title: NFPA 70 — Hazardous Locations: Class/Division and Class/Zone Classification
_note: Unified treatment of the two parallel classification systems used in NEC — Division (legacy, Arts 500-503) and Zone (IEC-aligned, Arts 505-506). Plus equipment selection matrix and seal placement rules.

classification_systems.name: Class/Division (NEC legacy)
classification_systems.scope: Arts 500-503. Class I (gas/vapor) × Division 1 (normal-condition ignitible concentration) / Division 2 (only abnormal). Class II (dust) × Division 1 / 2. Class III (ignitible fibers/flyings) × Division 1 / 2.

gas_groups.D: Propane, gasoline, methane, butane, ethanol, acetone, benzene, MEK

dust_groups.F: Coal dust, coke dust, carbon black
dust_groups.G: Grain dust, flour, plastic dust, wood dust

classification_systems.name: Class/Zone (IEC-aligned)
classification_systems.scope: Arts 505-506. Class I × Zone 0 (continuously present), Zone 1 (occasionally), Zone 2 (only abnormal). Zone 20/21/22 for dust.

gas_groups.IIC: ≈ NEC Groups A and B combined (acetylene, hydrogen)

severity_equivalence_matrix.severity_note: Division 1 covers both 'continuously present' AND 'occasionally present' — broader than Zone 1 alone

equipment_marking_examples.example: Class I, Div 1, Group D, T4
equipment_marking_examples.interpretation: Suitable for Class I (gases) Div 1 (continuous/occasional), Group D (propane/gasoline), T4 (max surface temp 135 °C)
Class I Zone 1 (occasional), Group IIA, T3 (max surface temp 200 °C)

equipment_marking_examples.example: II 2 G Ex db IIB+H2 T4 Gb
equipment_marking_examples.interpretation: Group II equipment, Category 2 (Zone 1), G (gas), explosion proof, IIB + hydrogen, T4 (135 °C), EPL Gb (high level protection)

protection_techniques_class_division.applicability: Class I Div 1, Class I Div 2
Class I Div 1 (uA), Class I Div 2
Class I Div 1 (Type X), Div 2 (Type Z)

protection_techniques_class_zone.applicability: Ex ia = Zone 0; Ex ib = Zone 1

seal_placement_rules.rule_summary: Conduit seal required at the boundary between hazardous and non-hazardous locations, and at certain equipment entries inside the hazardous location
seal_placement_rules.boundary_seal: Required where raceway leaves a hazardous location and enters a non-hazardous location
seal_placement_rules.equipment_seal: Required at conduit entries to: (1) Class I Div 1 enclosures containing arcing/sparking devices, (2) Class I Div 1 enclosures with internal volume > 100 in³
seal_placement_rules.conductor_seal_compound: Listed sealing compound; cannot use the EGC continuity through the seal — bond around with external bonding jumper

temperature_class_table._source: NEC 500.8(D) / 505.9(D) — equipment maximum surface temperature codes

common_errors: Mixing Division-marked and Zone-marked equipment in the same location — confirm equipment listing covers both or use one system throughout
Specifying T1 equipment in a hazardous location where the gas autoignition is 200 °C — must specify T3 or lower (lower number = lower surface temp = safer)
Omitting the boundary seal — frequent inspection failure on retrofits where existing raceway crosses a newly classified boundary
Treating gas Group IIC (acetylene + hydrogen) as covered by Group D (propane) equipment — IIC requires different equipment construction
_consuming_skills: Skills designing for industrial / chemical / fuel-dispensing facilities load this file alongside art500-506-hazardous-locations.json.
